diagrams.title: Product Governance Loop
diagrams.summary: Odylith governs its own product backlog, plans, bugs, diagrams, and generated surfaces inside the public repo while consumers keep their own repo truth locally.

components.name: odylith
components.description: Top-level product contract that owns the installable CLI, governance loop, and self-governing repo boundary.

components.name: dashboard
components.description: Shell surface that links the Odylith governance views into one operator-facing product entrypoint.

components.name: radar
components.description: Backlog/workstream surface that tracks active product slices and ranking decisions for Odylith itself.

components.name: atlas
components.description: Diagram surface that stores and renders the architecture maps governing the Odylith product.

components.name: compass
components.description: Execution-audit surface that records and renders Odylith timeline evidence for its own repo.

components.name: registry
components.description: Component evidence surface that ties Odylith product components to specs, workstreams, and conversation traces.

components.name: casebook
components.description: Bug and learning surface that captures Odylith product incidents and corrective follow-through.

diagrams.title: Context And Agent Execution Stack
diagrams.summary: Context and execution stack showing repo truth flowing through compiler-owned memory read models, the Context-to-Execution handshake, shared Execution Engine snapshot, visible intervention broker, and accuracy-first benchmark proof before routing, orchestration, or chatter act.

components.name: odylith-context-engine
components.description: Grounding engine that compiles repo truth into deterministic memory read models and retained packets.

components.name: memory substrate
components.description: Projection bundle, snapshot fallback, local backend, remote retrieval, and packet contracts are explicit siblings instead of one vague memory box.

components.name: delivery and diagnosis
components.description: Delivery Intelligence, Proof State, and Tribunal convert grounded truth into posture, blocker, and diagnosis inputs instead of directly controlling the next action.

components.name: execution engine and agent control
components.description: Execution Engine emits one compact snapshot that gates routing, orchestration, remediation, intervention visibility, and benchmark acceptance.

diagrams.title: Diagnosis And Remediation Loop
diagrams.summary: Tribunal and Remediator loop showing Context Engine packet evidence, Execution Engine admissibility, memory/session proof, visible intervention risk signals, and benchmark closure checks flowing through diagnosis, packet assembly, validation, and rollback posture.

components.name: tribunal
components.description: Diagnosis engine that weighs grounded evidence and produces ranked explanations, risks, and next checks.

components.name: remediator
components.description: Correction-packet compiler that turns approved diagnoses into bounded execution, validation, and rollback instructions.

diagrams.title: Delivery Governance Topology
diagrams.summary: Topology view of the Odylith observer, Context Engine packet digest, Execution Engine snapshot, Tribunal reasoning engine, Remediator packet flow, visible intervention broker, benchmark closure proof, runtime v4 artifacts, and native-evidence surfaces.

components.name: Odylith
components.description: Observer daemon and control-plane surface that owns the full operator queue, approvals, recommendations, and verification.

components.name: Registry
components.description: Component intelligence surface that stays native to component evidence, spec traceability, and forensic timeline.

components.name: Casebook
components.description: Bug knowledge surface that stays native to bug history, root cause, solution, verification, and prevention evidence.

components.name: Compass
components.description: Timeline evidence source that stays native to execution chronology, current workstreams, and risk framing.

components.name: Radar
components.description: Workstream requirement and success-metric surface that stays native to backlog evidence and traceability.

components.name: Atlas
components.description: Architecture surface that stays native to topology, freshness, and linked engineering artifacts.

components.name: Dashboard
components.description: Shared tooling shell that owns the top-3 Operator Inbox drawer and deep-link coherence across governance surfaces.

diagrams.title: Delivery Intelligence Closed Loop
diagrams.summary: Sequence view of the observe, delivery, Context Engine packet, Execution Engine snapshot, Tribunal, visible intervention, benchmark proof, packetize, approval, apply/delegate, and clearance loop after the v0.1.11 alignment work.

components.name: Odylith
components.description: Continuous observer loop that owns delta detection, correlation, posture persistence, queue refresh, and surface refresh.

components.name: Registry
components.description: Supplies component scope and proof routing context for queue items that land on live components.

components.name: Compass
components.description: Receives verification evidence and contributes timeline proof for queue items.

components.name: Dashboard
components.description: Hosts the tabbed operator surfaces plus the top-ranked shell inbox refreshed after posture changes.

diagrams.title: Operator Inbox and Evidence Routing
diagrams.summary: Focused routing diagram showing how Tribunal outcomes, Execution Engine snapshots, chat-visible intervention decisions, Remediator packets, and benchmark proof routes reach the shell inbox and Odylith while Radar, Registry, Casebook, Atlas, and Compass remain native evidence surfaces.

components.name: Operator queue
components.description: Ranked interventions with why_now, do_this_now, success_check, and proof_highlights.

components.name: Shell inbox
components.description: Viewport-owned top-3 drawer that surfaces ranked interventions and deep-links into Odylith.

components.name: Native evidence surfaces
components.description: Radar, Registry, Casebook, Atlas, and Compass stay native to their own evidence model and only route operators into Odylith when deeper intervention detail is needed.

diagrams.title: Tribunal Multi-Actor Reasoning Engine
diagrams.summary: Detailed Tribunal anatomy showing dossier intake enriched by Context Engine packets, Execution Engine snapshots, session memory, and delivery-ledger proof, then actor adjudication, intervention hints, benchmark risk signals, and grounded non-generic editor output.

components.name: Grounded dossier intake
components.description: Decision scope, named artifacts, timestamps, components, surfaces, approvals, and clearance state are normalized into one grounded Tribunal dossier.

components.name: Independent actor pass
components.description: Observer, ownership, causal, policy, normative, adversary, counterfactual, gap, risk, and prescriber all examine the same dossier before adjudication.

components.name: Adjudication and editorial merge
components.description: The adjudicator chooses the leading and rival explanations, then the editor compresses that into one maintainer-facing decision brief plus systemic context.

components.name: Reasoning guardrails
components.description: Grounding, non-generic output, self-reference downgrade, proof routing, and fingerprint-gated cache reuse constrain the actor system.

diagrams.title: Tribunal Dossier and Remediation Lifecycle
diagrams.summary: Detailed lifecycle view of dossier assembly with Context Engine packet, Execution Engine snapshot, and memory/session summaries, followed by actor adjudication, visible intervention signal emission, packet compilation, approval, execution or delegation, benchmark closure proof, and final clearance update.

components.name: Dossier builder
components.description: Delivery intelligence assembles one grounded case dossier with repo objects, checkpoints, approvals, and evidence before the actor pass starts.

components.name: Tribunal adjudication
components.description: Actor memos are merged into one leading explanation, one rival explanation, and an editorial brief rather than leaking raw actor chatter to Odylith.

components.name: Remediator packet engine
components.description: Executable prescriptions become bounded deterministic, AI-engine, hybrid, or manual packets with fingerprints, validation, and rollback semantics.

components.name: Approval and clearance loop
components.description: Operator approval, execution or delegation, stale checks, and clearance updates drive the final posture back into Odylith runtime state.

diagrams.title: Delivery Intelligence Signal Journal and Observer Pipeline
diagrams.summary: End-to-end view of Odylith signal intake, session and delivery-ledger journaling, Context Engine packet digests, Execution Engine snapshot reuse, visibility ledgers, Tribunal/remediator reasoning, visible intervention decisions, benchmark proof, and runtime artifacts feeding the maintainer control plane.

components.name: Signal sources
components.description: Repo worktree changes, Compass timeline events, and runtime-local build/CI/deploy/precommit signals all feed Odylith's observer loop.

components.name: Signal Journal
components.description: The runtime-local `events.v1.jsonl` journal records normalized non-Compass signals so the observer loop can correlate them on each refresh.

components.name: Observer and scope synthesis
components.description: The watcher builds the cheap posture shell, then Delivery Intelligence synthesizes the full scope universe and marks live-actionable scopes before Tribunal starts.

components.name: Runtime truth and consumers
components.description: Odylith persists posture, reasoning, delivery, and decision-ledger artifacts that drive the CLI, Odylith UI, Atlas proof routes, and full sync workflows.

diagrams.title: Tribunal Selection Funnel and Queue Formation
diagrams.summary: Selection-funnel view showing how Delivery Intelligence combines scope snapshots with Context Engine packet quality, Execution Engine posture, memory/session visibility gaps, and benchmark risk before Tribunal live-actionability, scenario coverage, and priority fill form the bounded maintainer queue.

components.name: Observed scope universe
components.description: Delivery Intelligence synthesizes workstream, component, diagram, surface, and grid scopes before Tribunal begins narrowing them.

components.name: Eligibility gate
components.description: Tribunal only considers live-actionable workstreams/components/diagrams whose primary scenario is not `clear_path`.

components.name: Selection contract
components.description: Tribunal ranks candidates, covers distinct scenario classes first, then fills remaining slots by priority while keeping stable tie-break semantics explicit.

components.name: Queue rationale output
components.description: The bounded queue carries both queue-level funnel stats and per-case inclusion rationale so truncation remains auditable.

diagrams.title: Tribunal Adjudication, Editorial Merge, and Packet Assembly
diagrams.summary: Detailed view of one selected Tribunal case moving from packet, execution-snapshot, session-proof, and evidence intake to actor memos, adjudication, provider validation, editorial merge, visible intervention signal, packet compilation, benchmark acceptance, and control-plane feedback.

components.name: Grounded dossier and actor pass
components.description: Tribunal builds one dossier and runs the full actor lattice over the same evidence before any editorial merge occurs.

components.name: Adjudication and provider validation
components.description: The adjudicator chooses the leading story and bounded provider refinements are accepted only when they validate against named evidence ids.

components.name: Editorial merge
components.description: Tribunal compresses the adjudicated outcome into a short queue headline and lead brief while keeping systemic and rival/risk context as separate outputs.

components.name: Packet assembly and feedback loop
components.description: Remediator compiles the bounded packet and Odylith later folds approval, delegation, apply, and clearance state back into posture.

diagrams.title: Subagent Router Decision and Escalation Flow
diagrams.summary: Decision flow for bounded subagent routing beneath the prompt-level orchestrator: intake, measured Odylith control-advisory assessment, graph-backed architecture-execution guards, confidence-backed profile selection, native spawn/close payload emission, rescope-vs-escalate outcomes, and family-aware adaptive tuning.

components.name: Route intake and assessment
components.description: The router normalizes bounded prompt metadata, scores ambiguity/risk/scope/validation signals, and assigns task family plus route confidence before delegation.

components.name: Measured control-advisory layer
components.description: The router now reads one bounded Odylith advisory contract compiled from the local evaluation ledger, carrying measured state/confidence plus depth/delegation/parallelism/budget/retrieval/speed posture, and also respects architecture-dossier execution hints when topology grounding is weak or high-risk.

components.name: Profile selection, confidence backstop, and xhigh gate
components.description: Weighted profile scoring chooses among Spark and GPT-5.4 tiers, low-confidence writes can be promoted to stronger GPT-5.4 profiles, grounded high-risk architecture slices can earn the strongest bounded lane, and `xhigh` remains separately gated by critical risk or maximum-accuracy feature conditions.

components.name: Validation, rescope, and adaptive loop
components.description: One spawned subagent is validated explicitly, may escalate once, may instead return control to the main thread for local rescope, and now emits native spawn-agent plus closeout payloads that keep the host thread aligned with the routed contract.

diagrams.title: Subagent Router Governance and Audit Topology
diagrams.summary: Governance topology for the Subagent Router as the bounded leaf-routing layer beneath the Subagent Orchestrator, including thin skills, route-confidence outputs, native spawn/close payloads, the local evaluation-to-advisory control loop, Compass audit stream, Registry manifest, living spec, runbook, and Atlas coverage.

components.name: Operator and execution surface
components.description: The main agent or maintainer invokes the thin skill and router script, which choose one worker profile for bounded delegation and emit route-confidence plus native host-call payloads.

components.name: Local state and forensic audit
components.description: Local evaluation and tuning state plus optional Compass timeline events preserve explainability, measured control advisories, family-aware adaptation, and feedback without creating tracked runtime truth.

components.name: Registry and Atlas governance
components.description: The component manifest, living spec, runbook, and diagram metadata make the routing policy inspectable across Registry, Atlas, Compass, and the tooling shell as a governed AI execution surface.

diagrams.title: Subagent Orchestrator Prompt Triage and Routing Flow
diagrams.summary: Prompt-level orchestration flow from an evidence-grounded repo-work prompt across the consumer lane, pinned dogfood, and detached `source-local` maintainer-dev posture through measured Odylith control advisories, architecture-dossier execution hints, local-only gates, orchestration-mode selection, and per-leaf handoff into the bounded Subagent Router, including native spawn and closeout payloads for host execution.

components.name: Evidence grounding and request shaping
components.description: The main thread grounds the evidence cone first, then converts the substantive grounded repo-work prompt into an orchestration request with candidate paths, acceptance criteria, validation commands, and accuracy posture.

components.name: Prompt triage and orchestration mode selection
components.description: The orchestrator evaluates hard local-only gates, measured control advisories, architecture execution hints, and conservative parallel-safety rules to choose `local_only`, `single_leaf`, `serial_batch`, or `parallel_batch`.

components.name: Leaf-routing handoff
components.description: Every delegated leaf is routed through Subagent Router for explicit model plus reasoning selection, while the orchestrator keeps merge ownership and now carries native spawn-agent plus closeout payloads, compact control-advisory posture, and architecture risk/fanout hints in the main-thread execution plan.

diagrams.title: Subagent Orchestrator Safe Fan-Out and Parallel Safety
diagrams.summary: Parallel-safety decision tree for automatic orchestration, including read-only fan-out caps, disjoint-write checks, coupled-path downgrades, native per-leaf spawn/close payloads, and the local fallback path when any leaf becomes unsafe.

components.name: Safety classification
components.description: The orchestrator classifies grounded prompts into `read_only_safe`, `disjoint_write_safe`, `serial_ordered`, or `local_only` based on path ownership, criticality, and validation clarity.

components.name: Conservative batch shaping
components.description: Read-only work may split into a small parallel batch, disjoint writes may split into a smaller capped batch, and coupled docs/tests/skills/contracts/tooling work is downgraded to serial or local execution.

components.name: Leaf fallback and safety override
components.description: Even after prompt-level decomposition, any leaf-router `main_thread` refusal collapses the whole orchestration plan back to local rescope instead of forcing unsafe fan-out, and accepted leaves still carry native spawn/close payloads for the main thread.

diagrams.title: Subagent Orchestrator Merge Barrier and Adjudication Ownership
diagrams.summary: Main-thread ownership model for serial and parallel orchestration, covering dependency barriers, cross-leaf merge responsibility, local rescope fallbacks, native closeout payload application, and the rule that architectural adjudication never belongs to a worker leaf.

components.name: Serial dependency ownership
components.description: Ordered leaves expose explicit dependency ids so the main thread controls when the next leaf may start and whether earlier results require local correction first.

components.name: Parallel merge barrier
components.description: Parallel leaves run only on disjoint owned paths, but the main thread still owns merge, final validation, cross-leaf acceptance, and delegated-leaf closeout before any result is treated as landed.

components.name: Rescope and adjudication fallback
components.description: If a leaf route or escalation reveals broader coordination, design conflict, or unclear ownership, control returns to the main thread rather than bouncing the ambiguity between workers.

diagrams.title: Subagent Orchestrator Governance and Adaptive Learning Topology
diagrams.summary: Governance topology for automatic orchestration across repo policy, thin skills, Registry and Atlas linkage, native host payload contracts, local adaptive learning state, and optional Compass audit so prompt-level delegation remains inspectable and bounded.

components.name: Policy and operator surface
components.description: Root agent policy, agent guidelines, runbooks, and thin skills define when orchestration is automatic, when routing remains leaf-only, and how maintainers should interpret native spawn/close payloads alongside commit-ready vs clean-snapshot proof.

components.name: Local adaptive state and audit
components.description: The orchestrator stores local-only replay-safe tuning by mode and task family, and may append Compass runtime events without turning local adaptation into tracked repo truth.

components.name: Registry and Atlas governance
components.description: The Registry manifest, living specs, runbooks, and diagram catalog make automatic orchestration a first-class governed component linked to the router, Atlas, Registry, and shell evidence surfaces.

diagrams.title: Installable Product Layered Topology
diagrams.summary: Layered topology for the installable Odylith product showing tracked truth, governed memory/session streams, Context Engine packets, Execution Engine snapshots, Tribunal/remediator benchmark proof, visible intervention broker, conversation intelligence, governed surfaces, and optional remote augmentation without consumer-specific product branches.

components.name: Tracked product truth and local state
components.description: Keeps repo-owned Radar, Registry, Atlas, Casebook, technical plans, and docs separate from mutable `.odylith/` runtime state.

components.name: Local-first product layers
components.description: Shows the CLI, context engine, governed memory family, delivery or diagnosis engines, conversation intelligence, and surface-runtime layers that compose the Odylith product.

components.name: Optional remote augmentation
components.description: Makes sparse recall and control attachments explicit as optional edges rather than hidden requirements for local-first operation.

diagrams.title: Installable Product Bootstrap and Validation Flow
diagrams.summary: Bootstrap and validation flow for Odylith showing consumer-profile resolution, local-first runtime initialization, surface materialization, and the proof gates that keep install and sync honest.

components.name: Bootstrap lane
components.description: Shows the `odylith install` or `odylith sync` path through consumer-profile resolution and runtime bootstrap.

components.name: Governed surface materialization
components.description: Shows where governance, evaluation, and agent-control evidence land in the rendered product surfaces.

components.name: Validation gates
components.description: Makes the product proof boundary explicit so surface coherence, packet contracts, and execution payloads are checked together.

diagrams.title: Product Runtime Boundary Map
diagrams.summary: Runtime boundary map for Odylith showing how product truth, governed memory, Context Engine packets, Execution Engine snapshots, intervention visibility ledgers, benchmark proof, generated surfaces, `.odylith` state, and separate runtime/write/validation boundaries fit together.

components.name: Product truth inputs
components.description: Names the product-owned source roots and contracts that drive the runtime instead of flattening them into one generic repo box.

components.name: Governed memory and execution modules
components.description: Separates context compilation, the governed memory family, delivery intelligence, evaluation, conversation intelligence, and surface modules as the live executable product boundary.

components.name: Generated outputs and local state
components.description: Shows how materialized `odylith/` surfaces, compiler artifacts, memory state, and mutable `.odylith/` runtime state differ while still feeding the same governed runtime loop.

diagrams.title: Self-Host Runtime And Release Gate
diagrams.summary: Self-host posture map showing the installed consumer lane, the Odylith product repo's maintainer mode with pinned dogfood versus detached source-local dev posture, the separate runtime/write/validation boundaries, and the release gate that only accepts pinned dogfood proof.

components.name: Pinned dogfood lane
components.description: Shows the main product repo operating against its tracked pinned runtime rather than implicitly running from a live checkout.

components.name: Detached dev lane
components.description: Makes source-local work an explicit separate override with loud release-ineligible posture instead of a hidden default.

components.name: Release gate and readouts
components.description: Shows the source-only validator and the shell and Compass self-host readouts that expose product-repo drift.

diagrams.title: Canonical Release Version Session And Publish Lane
diagrams.summary: Canonical maintainer release lane showing preview and sticky session resolution, commit-bound dispatch into the GitHub workflow, signed asset publication, and the post-publish dogfood plus consumer proof sequence.

components.name: Sticky release session
components.description: Shows how one local session version and head SHA are resolved once and then reused across preflight and dispatch.

components.name: Canonical publish lane
components.description: Shows the repo, actor, branch, clean-worktree, and commit-binding guards that keep the maintained release lane authoritative.

components.name: Post-publish proof
components.description: Shows the dogfood activation and consumer rehearsal steps that turn a published version into a credible release candidate.

diagrams.title: Managed Runtime Release And Install Flow
diagrams.summary: Supported-platform release and install flow showing managed runtime bundle publication, signed verification, and strict interpreter separation between Odylith and the consumer repo toolchain.

components.name: Managed runtime bundles
components.description: Canonical releases ship platform-specific Odylith-managed Python runtimes for macOS and Linux.

components.name: Installer verification
components.description: The hosted installer selects the current supported platform, verifies signed assets, and stages the runtime under .odylith.

components.name: Interpreter boundary
components.description: Odylith commands run inside .odylith while consumer repo commands stay on the consumer toolchain with no shell-level switching.

diagrams.title: Benchmark Proof And Publication Lane
diagrams.summary: Maps the benchmark corpus, full-product Odylith-on versus raw-agent proof lanes, Context Engine handshake metrics, Execution Engine snapshot accuracy, chat-visible intervention transcript proof, memory/Tribunal influence, conservative publication logic, and release outputs with accuracy gates ahead of latency and token-budget gates.

components.name: Benchmark
components.description: Benchmark runner, corpus, conservative publication view, and graph pipeline that turn Odylith benchmark data into release-safe proof.

components.name: Odylith Context Engine
components.description: Grounding, packet quality, target resolution, and route-readiness behavior measured as part of full-product assistance.

components.name: Execution and intervention proof
components.description: Execution Engine snapshot accuracy, delegation guards, false-admit/false-deny rates, and chat-visible Observation/Proposal/Assist proof are measured before publication.

diagrams.title: Memory Substrate Compile, Retrieval, and Packet Topology
diagrams.summary: Shows how compiler-owned projection artifacts, local and optional remote retrieval, session streams, delivery-ledger visibility proof, and packet-safe memory contracts feed Context Engine packets, Execution Engine snapshots, Tribunal summaries, visible intervention decisions, and benchmark proof.

components.name: Compiler read models
components.description: Projection bundle and snapshot are compiler-owned read models that make downstream memory deterministic.

components.name: Retrieval lanes
components.description: Local backend and optional remote retrieval consume compiler outputs without becoming source-of-truth writers.

components.name: Packet contract boundary
components.description: Memory contracts sanitize, compact, and carry retained memory, session, proof, and visibility summaries into runtime consumers.

components.name: Alignment consumers
components.description: Execution Engine, Tribunal, visible intervention broker, router/orchestrator, chatter, and benchmark proof consume compact memory summaries instead of scanning full stores on the hot path.

diagrams.title: Live Proof-State Control Plane
diagrams.summary: Shows how tracked blocker truth and runtime live-proof memory resolve into one falsifiable proof lane that feeds Delivery Intelligence, Tribunal, Context Engine packets, Execution Engine snapshots, visible intervention warnings, shell or Compass surfaces, benchmark acceptance, and claim lint.

components.name: Tracked proof truth
components.description: Casebook bugs and bound plans define blocker identity, first failing phase, and clearance rule when that metadata is present.

components.name: Runtime live-proof memory
components.description: The proof-surfaces ledger retains repeated fingerprints, last falsification, deployment truth, and frontier movement for one lane_id.

components.name: Shared proof consumers
components.description: Delivery, Tribunal, packets, surfaces, and chatter all consume the same resolved proof-state lane instead of inventing separate summaries.

diagrams.title: Release Planning And Workstream Targeting
diagrams.summary: Shows how release registry truth, append-only workstream assignment history, and release-note title inheritance flow through validation, authoring, traceability, Radar, Compass, Context Engine, and maintainer release guidance.

components.name: Release source truth
components.description: The release registry and assignment event log own immutable release ids, aliases, lifecycle state, and auditable workstream targeting.

components.name: Release planning contract
components.description: Shared selector resolution, lifecycle validation, and release-note name alignment keep every consumer on one truthful release lane.

components.name: Release consumers
components.description: Traceability, Radar, Compass, Context Engine, and the maintainer runbook all read the same release-planning contract.

diagrams.title: Scope Signal Ladder And Escalation Topology
diagrams.summary: Shows the shared deterministic ladder that turns raw scope evidence, Execution Engine posture, visibility-ledger proof, and benchmark risk into one additive contract for visibility, promotion, execution budget, and expensive compute budgets across governance surfaces.

components.name: Scope feature intake
components.description: Timeline events, local path classes, plan status, operator readouts, and proof posture collapse into one deterministic feature vector.

components.name: Scope Signal Ladder
components.description: One shared rung contract assigns visibility, promotion, and budget posture instead of letting each surface invent urgency locally.

components.name: Provider-neutral budgets
components.description: The ladder emits host-agnostic budget classes so Codex, Claude, and future providers all follow one product compute policy.

diagrams.title: Governance Surface Focus And Budget Gating Flow
diagrams.summary: Shows how one scope rung drives Compass quiet-vs-promoted behavior, Radar default ordering, Atlas and Registry promotion, Execution Engine profile selection, visible-intervention fail-visible policy, benchmark cost gates, and host-neutral compute budgets without changing exhaustive source truth.

components.name: Quiet low-signal path
components.description: Suppressed or background scopes remain deep-linkable but stay quiet instead of borrowing activity from global context.

components.name: Promoted default focus
components.description: Verified local, active, actionable, and blocker scopes rise through Compass, Radar, Registry, and Atlas without changing source completeness.

components.name: Budget gating flow
components.description: The same rung decides whether a scope gets no spend, cache-only reuse, cheap local reasoning, or escalated fresh provider work.

diagrams.title: Execution Engine Stack
diagrams.summary: Shows the execution-engine package consuming Context Engine handshake v1, delivery posture, proof-state signals, Tribunal diagnosis, and memory/session truth to emit one shared snapshot for admissibility, frontier, closure, receipts, validation, router/orchestrator guards, visible interventions, runtime surfaces, and benchmark proof.

components.name: Execution contract core
components.description: Host profile, task contract, Context Engine handshake, and admissibility policy define the shared execution-engine state and first hard action gate.

components.name: Frontier and closure layer
components.description: Frontier, resource closure, and semantic receipts keep execution truthful, resumable, and safer than command validity alone.

components.name: Downstream execution surfaces
components.description: Router, orchestrator, remediator, Compass, shell, packets, visible intervention broker, dashboard, and benchmarks consume the same execution snapshot instead of re-deriving policy locally.

diagrams.title: Program/Wave Authoring And Agent Command Flow
diagrams.summary: Shows the thin program/wave authoring sidecar letting coding agents maintain umbrella execution-wave source truth through direct commands rather than hand-editing JSON.

components.name: Direct authoring commands
components.description: Coding agents use `odylith program ...` and `odylith wave ...` as the ergonomic layer over the canonical source contract.

components.name: Canonical program file
components.description: The execution-wave JSON file remains the one source of truth and is validated before downstream consumers render or query it.

components.name: Shared consumers
components.description: Radar, Compass, Context Engine, and validators all consume the same program file rather than a second planning schema.

diagrams.title: Compass Refresh And Maintained Narration Topology
diagrams.summary: Shows the one bounded Compass refresh contract from operator entry through fact rebuild, scope-signal gating, narrated cache reuse, cold-start maintenance warming, and the edge cases that must fail closed instead of reviving a second expensive refresh lane.

components.name: Bounded refresh runtime
components.description: The canonical refresh command rebuilds facts, scope signals, and reuse fingerprints through one shell-safe lane instead of branching into a second deep-refresh mode.

components.name: Scoped and global brief decisions
components.description: Global windows stay cache-first and bounded, while scoped windows split into quiet, cache or deterministic, or fresh-provider paths based on verified activity and ladder rung.

components.name: Maintained narrated-cache sidecar
components.description: A deduped background worker warms only the missing high-value narrated briefs and patches the current runtime snapshot only when its input fingerprint still matches.

diagrams.title: Claude Guidance Companion Surface Topology
diagrams.summary: Shows the AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md companion surface model across the repo root and odylith/ subtree, including ownership (repo-owned canonical vs product-managed companion), root guidance detection accepting either marker, and the runtime classification that makes CLAUDE.md first-class guidance across all heuristics.

components.name: Cross-host canonical contract
components.description: AGENTS.md files at the repo root and odylith/ subtree are the canonical cross-host policy, authored by maintainers and never overwritten by Odylith install or repair.

components.name: Claude-native companion surfaces
components.description: CLAUDE.md files at the repo root and odylith/ subtree are managed by Odylith install and repair as companion entrypoints that defer to the sibling AGENTS.md as canonical truth.

components.name: Root guidance detection and runtime classification
components.description: Install and runtime now accept either AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md as a valid root guidance marker, and classify CLAUDE.md as guidance (not ordinary docs) across workstream inference, dirty-overlap, benchmark isolation, consumer profile, and context routing.

diagrams.title: Claude Guidance Install And Repair Flow
diagrams.summary: Sequence diagram for the odylith install and repair flow extended for B-083: shows root guidance detection accepting either AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md, the guidance-on/off toggle, additive materialization of root and odylith/ CLAUDE.md companion files, consumer profile rebuild, and the proof gate that confirms the companion surfaces are coherent with the canonical AGENTS.md contract.

components.name: Root guidance detection
components.description: Guidance detector probes both AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md at the repo root and accepts either as a valid marker, so a repo with only CLAUDE.md can bootstrap correctly.

components.name: Companion materialization
components.description: Under guidance-on (default), installer writes or refreshes root CLAUDE.md and odylith/CLAUDE.md as managed companion files without touching the repo-owned AGENTS.md files.

components.name: Validation gate
components.description: Proof harness confirms AGENTS.md is intact, companion files are materialized and deferring to canonical truth, before accepting the install as coherent.

diagrams.title: Claude Guidance Runtime Heuristic Integration
diagrams.summary: Shows how a shared is_guidance_file() classifier extended for B-083 makes CLAUDE.md first-class guidance across five runtime heuristics — workstream inference, dirty-overlap, benchmark isolation, consumer profile, and context routing — with explicit before and after outcome comparison.

components.name: Shared guidance classifier
components.description: A single is_guidance_file() predicate returns true for both AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md, providing a consistent source of truth for all downstream heuristics.

components.name: Five extended heuristics
components.description: workstream_inference, dirty_overlap, benchmark_runner, consumer_profile, and tooling_context_routing all consume the classifier instead of hard-coding AGENTS.md path checks.

components.name: Before and after outcome comparison
components.description: Explicit before-B-083 and after-B-083 outcome nodes show what breaks when CLAUDE.md is treated as ordinary docs and what correct behavior looks like after the fix.

diagrams.title: Governed Sync Shared Read Model and Incremental Derivation Loop
diagrams.summary: Shows how governed sync hoists shared read models for Context Engine, Execution Engine snapshots, memory/session visibility ledgers, Tribunal, surfaces, and benchmark checks, deriving validator and renderer work from compact cached truth while avoiding unchanged output churn.

components.name: Odylith
components.description: Owns the sync execution engine, canonical derivation contract, and content-addressed write discipline.

components.name: Radar
components.description: Provides backlog and plan source truth reused across validators and renderers.

components.name: Registry
components.description: Provides component inventory and dossier truth reused by Registry sync and delivery intelligence.

components.name: Dashboard
components.description: Consumes the derived surface payloads and should not force fake downstream invalidation when bytes did not change.

diagrams.title: Context Engine Connection Lifecycle and Process Topology
diagrams.summary: Shows the current stateless open-close connection model, the existing daemon gap, planned connection and packet-summary caches, Execution Engine snapshot reuse, visibility-status cache, benchmark latency and token-budget checks, and the multi-repo/multi-session safety contract.

components.name: Memory Backend
components.description: Owns the LanceDB and Tantivy connection lifecycle and the planned connection cache.

components.name: Context Engine
components.description: Owns the daemon serve loop, the dispatch handler, and the query routing decision between direct-process and daemon-backed paths.

diagrams.title: Conversation Observation And Governed Proposal Flow
diagrams.summary: Shows how Codex and Claude conversation evidence, Context Engine packet summaries, Execution Engine snapshots, memory/session stream, Tribunal signals, Casebook/Radar/Registry/Atlas anchors, and delivery-ledger proof flow through the proposition ledger, hard gates, deterministic value features, conflict graph, subset optimizer, visibility broker, ruled renderer, Compass decision log, and bootstrap adjudication report before any Odylith Risks, History, Insight, Observation, Proposal, or Assist reaches chat.

components.name: governance-intervention-engine
components.description: Shared cross-host core, proposition-first value engine, subset selector, renderer contract, and visibility broker that turns live session evidence into governed, chat-visible Odylith blocks.

components.name: odylith-chatter
components.description: Owns the voice and rendering contract for human Odylith narration.

components.name: compass
components.description: Owns the audit stream, selected/suppressed proposition decision log, visibility proof state, and derived pending proposal state.

components.name: execution-engine
components.description: Supplies the compact execution summary that influences timing, actionability, closure, recovery, and visibility need without owning visible selection.

components.name: Context, memory, Tribunal, and benchmark proof
components.description: Packet summaries, session/delivery ledger state, Tribunal signals, bootstrap corpus cases, advisory selector reports, and transcript harnesses prove mechanism behavior without claiming ML calibration or full odylith_on outcome lift.

diagrams.title: Discipline Loop
diagrams.summary: Shows the credit-safe Odylith Discipline loop from local pressure observations through stance, hard laws, affordances, proof, compact learning, Tribunal doctrine, governed surfaces, benchmark evidence, and updated priors.
